commands.repair_reason: Needs contributor real-behavior proof and maintainer sequencing review; no narrow automated code repair is identified.; Cleared: No concrete security or supply-chain regression was found; the PR commit touches Doctor source/tests only and Socket reported no dependency changes. commands.review_summary:
The branch adds a shell completion health check, routes shell completion repair through the structured Doctor repair runner at its existing position, filters it out of the broad repair pass, and adds focused tests.

Reproducibility: not applicable. this is a refactor/cleanup PR rather than a bug report. Source review shows the changed shell completion repair path, but I did not run a live Doctor CLI scenario because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: Needs contributor real-behavior proof and maintainer sequencing review; no narrow automated code repair is identified.

target.title: Doctor: structure shell completion repair
target.body:
## Summary

- Converts shell completion cache/profile repair into a structured core doctor health check.
- Keeps shell completion positional: broad structured repairs skip it, then the existing shell-completion contribution runs the structured check at its original point in doctor order.
- Preserves the legacy optional install prompt path only when the structured check reports no finding, so stale cache/profile repairs are not run twice.
- Keeps optional missing shell completion quiet for lint and non-interactive repair, and makes no plugin, gateway, SDK, or public health contract changes.

## Verification

- pnpm install
- node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/commands/doctor-completion.ts src/commands/doctor-completion.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts
- git diff --check
- node_modules/.bin/oxlint src/commands/doctor-completion.ts src/commands/doctor-completion.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts
- node_modules/.bin/tsgo -p tsconfig.core.json --noEmit --incremental false --pretty false
- node_modules/.bin/tsgo -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --noEmit --incremental false --pretty false
- node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor-completion.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts src/flows/doctor-repair-flow.test.ts --reporter=dot: 4 files / 47 tests passed
- codex review --base origin/main: no discrete correctness issues

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Shell completion doctor repair now emits structured findings/repair results for slow dynamic completion and installed profiles with missing completion caches, without making optional missing completion a lint failure.
Real environment tested: WSL Ubuntu 24.04 root durable worktree /root/src/openclaw-85566-shell.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor-completion.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts src/flows/doctor-repair-flow.test.ts --reporter=dot
Evidence after fix: 4 files / 47 tests passed; tsgo core and core-test passed; oxfmt/oxlint/diff checks passed; Codex review clean.
Observed result after fix: Shell completion is excluded from the broad structured repair pass, runs at its original contribution position, avoids double-running actual structured repairs, keeps optional missing completion quiet in lint/non-interactive repair, and preserves the legacy optional install prompt path.
What was not tested: Full repo check; live mutation of a real user shell profile.
